key_insight: SLiR turns many activation functions into optimizable sound linear bounds by choosing a slope and then shifting the offset to guarantee valid upper and lower relaxations.
engineering_relevance: This makes neural network verification easier to scale because new activations need little manual relaxation design, while still supporting formal correctness guarantees.
method: Parameterize each relaxation by its slope.
Compute the offset with a shifting procedure that enforces sound bounds over the input domain.
Use only a Lipschitz constant or a set of critical points, so the method applies broadly to many activations.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports tight relaxations across a wide range of activation functions and up to 7.8× more verified properties than state-of-the-art methods.
watch_item: Performance depends on having a valid Lipschitz constant or critical points, and the shifting step must be implemented carefully to preserve soundness.
paper_title: Shifting-based Optimizable Linear Relaxations for General Activation Functions
